name: pychomp2
documentation:
# pyCHomP2

CHomP (Computational Homology Project) with Python bindings

## Installation

To get the latest tagged version from the PyPi repository:

```bash
pip install pychomp2
```

To uninstall:

```bash
pip uninstall pychomp2
```

## Installing from source

To get the bleeding edge version (i.e., this repo):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/marciogameiro/pyCHomP2.git
cd pyCHomP2
git submodule update --init --recursive
pip install . --ignore-installed --no-cache-dir -v -v -v --user
```

functions.name: pychomp.TopologicalSort.TopologicalSort
documentation:
Topological Sort of a directed acyclic graph (reverse ordered topsort)
Example:
  vertices = [1,2,3,4,5]
  edges = [(1,2),(2,3),(2,4),(4,5),(3,5),(1,5)]
  adjacencies = lambda v :  [ j for (i,j) in edges if i == v ]
  print(TopologicalSort(vertices,adjacencies))

functions.name: pychomp.StronglyConnectedComponents.StronglyConnectedComponents
documentation:
Overview:
    Compute the strongly connected components.
Inputs:
    vertices_input        : a collection of vertices
    adjacencies_input : a function which takes a vertex and gives the adjacency list

classes.name: pychomp.DirectedAcyclicGraph.DirectedAcyclicGraph

documentation: Return a graphviz string describing the graph and its labels
TODO: don't silently fail if given a non-DAG

functions.name: pychomp.FlowGradedComplex.FlowGradedComplex
documentation:
Overview:
  Given a complex and a graph on its top dimensional cells,
  produce a GradedComplex such that the preimage of a down set
  is the collection of cells in the closure of all the 
  associated top cells
Inputs:
  complex    : a complex
  flow_graph : a function from vertices to out-adjacent vertices
Algorithm:
  Apply strongly connected components algorithm and determine
  reachability relation among the strong components to learn
  a poset. Associated to each poset vertex is a collection of
  top cells.

functions.name: pychomp.Braids.BraidComplex
documentation:
Overview:
  Given a specification for a "braids" dynamical system,
  return the associated cubical complex and flow graph.

methods.name: pychomp.Braids.BraidDiagram.__init__
documentation:
Inputs:
  braid_skeleton : a list of lists such that 
     braid_skeleton[i][j] gives the value of strand i at position j
Outputs:
  braid_specification: a tuple (n, m, x, pi) 
                       where n : number of strands 
                             m : number of waypoints 
                             x(i,j) means the value of strand i at position j
                             pi is a permutation such that x(i,j+m) == x(pi(i),j)

methods.name: pychomp.Braids.BraidDiagram.__call__
documentation: Return the height of strand i at position j

methods.name: pychomp.Braids.BraidDiagram.pi
documentation: pi is a permutation such that x(i,j+m) == x(pi(i),j)

methods.name: pychomp.Braids.BraidDiagram.lap
documentation: Compute the lap number for a domain (given by a point in it)

functions.name: pychomp.Poset.LatticeOfDownsets
documentation: Generate from poset the Hasse diagram of the poset of down-sets of "poset" ordered by inclusion

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.__init__
documentation: Create a Poset P from a DAG G such that x <= y in P iff there is a path from y to x in G

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.__iter__
documentation: Allows for the semantics [v for v in poset]

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.vertices
documentation: Return the set of elements in the poset

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.parents
documentation: Return the immediate predecessors of v in the poset

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.children
documentation: Return the immediate successors of v in the poset

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.ancestors
documentation: Return the set { u : v < u }

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.descendants
documentation: Return the set { u : u < v }

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.less
documentation: Return True if u < v, False otherwise

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset.maximal
documentation: Return the set of elements in "subset" which are maximal

methods.name: pychomp.Poset.Poset._repr_svg_
documentation: Return svg representation for visual display

functions.name: pychomp.CubicalHomology.CubicalHomology
documentation:
Compute homology of a collection (list) of top dimensional
cubes and return the Betti numbers. Each cube in the list of
cubes is represented by its minimum vertex.
